info.title: Freestyle Sandboxes
info.description: Freestyle Sandboxes lets you deploy your users or AIs code. **Get your API Key at [admin.freestyle.sh](https://admin.freestyle.sh)**

get.description: Returns information about the currently authenticated user or identity.

post.description: Deploy a cloudstate project

get.description: Get a backup of a cloudstate project

post.description:
Provisions a wildcard certificate for a verified domain


This speeds up deploys on all subdomains of the domain. In order to use it, you must add the following record to your DNS config:

`_acme-challenge.yourdomain.com` NS `dns.freestyle.sh`

get.summary: List domain verification requests for an account
get.description: Lists domain verification requests for the current account.

x-codeSamples.label: JS SDK

put.description: This checks a pre-existing verification request for a domain. To create a verification request, call the [create domain verification](/#tag/domains/POST/domains/v1/verifications) endpoint. This endpoint will check if the domain has a TXT record with the verification code. If it does, the domain will be verified.

400.description: Possible errors: InvalidDomain, FailedToCreateVerificationCode, FailedToDeleteVerification, VerificationFailed

500.description: Possible errors: FailedToVerifyDomain, FailedToListVerifications, FailedToListDomains, FailedToInsertDomainMapping, InternalError

x-codeSamples.label: JS SDK

post.description: This creates a Freestyle Domain Verification Request. It returns a `verificationCode` for your domain. You need to place this code in a TXT record at `_freestyle_custom_hostname.thedomain.com`, then call the [verify domain](/#tag/domains/PUT/domains/v1/verifications) endpoint with the domain to verify it.

x-codeSamples.label: JS SDK

delete.description: This deletes a Freestyle Domain Verification Request. This does not remove the domain from the account if it has already been verified, however the verification code will no longer be valid.

x-codeSamples.label: JS SDK
Vercel AI SDK
Mastra AI SDK
LangGraph AI SDK
Python SDK
Gemini Python SDK
x-codeSamples.source:
import google.genai as genai
from google.genai import types

import os
import freestyle.gemini

client = genai.Client(api_key=os.environ.get("GENERATIVEAI_API_KEY"))


# model = genai.client.com("gemini-2.0-flash")
definition, runner = freestyle.gemini.execute_tool(
    os.environ.get("FREESTYLE_API_KEY"),
)
chat = client.chats.create(
    model="gemini-2.0-flash",
    config=types.GenerateContentConfigDict(
        tools=[definition],
    ),
    history=[],
)

response = chat.send_message(
    "What is the sum of every number from 50 to 65 divided by 17"
).candidates[0]
tool_result = runner(response.content)

print("Answer: ", tool_result)

x-codeSamples.label: OpenAI Python SDK
x-codeSamples.source:
import os
import openai
import freestyle.openai

client = openai.OpenAI(
    api_key=os.environ.get('OPENAI_API_KEY'),
)


(definition, runner) = freestyle.openai.execute_tool(
    os.environ.get('FREESTYLE_API_KEY'),
)

query = 'What is the sum of every number from 50 to 65 divided by 17'
messages = [{'role': 'user', 'content': query}]
print('Question: ', query)

res = client.chat.completions.create(
    model='gpt-4-turbo', messages=messages, tools=[definition]
)


result = runner(res.choices[0].message)

messages.append(res.choices[0].message)
if len(result) > 0:
    messages.extend(result)

res = client.chat.completions.create(
    model='gpt-4-turbo',
    messages=messages,
    tools=[definition],
)

print('Answer: ', res.choices[0].message.content)

post.description:
Create a repository. Once the repository is created, it will also be created on the Git server.
The repository name must be unique within your account.

Once created, you can then push your code to this repository.

The repo will be available at `git.freestyle.sh/{repo-id}`

name.description:
This name is not visible to users, and is only accessible to you via API and in the
dashboard. Mostly useful for observability.

defaultBranch.description: The default branch name for the repository. Defaults to "main" if not specified.

source.description: Fork from another Git repository. Cannot be used with `import`.

import.description: Import static content with an initial commit. Cannot be used with `source`.

post.description: Create a commit from files with automatic tree building and branch updates. Supports text files (UTF-8), binary files (base64), file deletions, and optimistic concurrency control via expectedSha. If the target branch does not exist, it is created as an orphan branch with this commit as its root.

get.description: Get a blob from the Git database. The contents will always be base64 encoded.

List commits with flexible filtering and pagination. Supports:
- Cursor-based pagination (recommended): Use `next_commit` from response with `until` (desc order) or `since` (asc order)
- Commit ranges: Specify both `since` and `until` to get commits between them (like git's `since..until`)
- Order control: `order=desc` (newest first, default) or `order=asc` (oldest first)
- Legacy offset pagination: Use `offset` param (deprecated, incompatible with since/until)

Requires repository visibility to be public or user to be the owner.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Maximum number of commits to return (default: 50, max: 500)

parameters.name: order
parameters.description: Sort order: "desc" (newest first, default) or "asc" (oldest first)

parameters.name: since
parameters.description:
Start point (older commit) for filtering/pagination (commit SHA)
- When used alone with order=desc: Returns commits newer than this (exclusive). Use for "what's new since X?"
- When used alone with order=asc: Starts from this commit (inclusive). Use with next_commit for pagination.
- When used with `until`: Defines the base of a range (like git's A..B where this is A)

parameters.name: until
parameters.description:
End point (newer commit) for filtering/pagination (commit SHA)
- When used alone with order=desc: Starts from this commit (inclusive). Use with next_commit for pagination.
- When used alone with order=asc: Returns commits up to and including this commit.
- When used with `since`: Defines the head of a range (like git's A..B where this is B)

When both `since` and `until` are provided, `until` must be a descendant of `since`.

post.summary: Create a commit from git objects
post.description: Create a commit from tree and parent SHAs. This is a git object database operation for advanced use cases. For file-based commits, use the high-level endpoint at /git/v1/repo/{repo}/commits.

get.description: Get a commit from the Git database with detailed information.
Get a list of all branches in the Git repository. Returns branch names and their commit SHAs.
Get a reference to a branch in the Git repository. Returns the ref name and SHA of the branch.

get.summary: List all tags in a repository
get.description:
List all tags in the Git repository with full details. Returns both annotated tags (with tagger and message)
        and lightweight tags. For each tag, the `sha` field contains the tag object SHA (for annotated tags) or
        commit SHA (for lightweight tags), while `target.sha` always contains the commit SHA the tag points to.

Get a tag by its name. This is the recommended endpoint for retrieving tag information.
        Works with both annotated tags (returns tagger, message, and target commit) and lightweight tags
        (returns only name and target commit). For annotated tags, the `sha` field contains the tag object's SHA,
        while `target.sha` contains the commit SHA. For lightweight tags, both fields contain the same commit SHA.

        **Note**: If you need to retrieve an annotated tag object by its SHA (for low-level git database operations),
        use `/git/v1/repo/{repo}/git/tags/{hash}` instead.

tagger.description: The tagger who created the tag

target.description: The object this tag points to

get.summary: Get an annotated tag object by SHA (advanced)
get.description:
Low-level endpoint for retrieving annotated tag objects directly from the Git database by their SHA hash.
        This endpoint only works with annotated tags and requires the tag object's SHA, not the tag name.


        **Most users should use `/git/v1/repo/{repo}/git/refs/tags/{tag}` instead**, which works with both
        annotated and lightweight tags and accepts tag names rather than SHAs. This endpoint is primarily
        for advanced use cases that need direct access to git database objects.
Get a tree from the Git database with its entries.

post.description: Create a new branch in the Git repository. If sha is not provided, the branch is created from the default branch HEAD.

AccessibleRepository.description: Identical to [`RepositoryInfo`], but with the permissions field added.

AccountId.description: Account identifier — either "system" or a user UUID

BaseImageSpec.description:
Specifies a Docker base image via inline Dockerfile content.
Used as an alternative to `docker_image` when you want to build
a custom image on the fly without a pre-published registry image.
No build context is supported yet.

dockerfileContent.description: The text content of a Dockerfile (e.g., `"FROM debian:trixie-slim"`).

BlobEncoding.description: The encoding of a blob from the API. Always `base64`.

content.description: The content of the blob, base64 encoded.

encoding.description: The encoding of the blob. Always `base64`.

commit.description: The latest commit ID on this branch. Null if the branch is empty.

date.description: Optional date (if not provided, uses current time)

CommitComparison.description: Response containing the comparison between two commits

ahead_by.description: Number of commits the head is ahead of base

behind_by.description: Number of commits the head is behind base

total_commits.description: Total number of commits in the comparison

count.description: Number of commits returned in this page

offset.description: Number of commits skipped (offset) - deprecated, use `since`/`until` selectors instead

total.description: Total number of commits available in the branch/range

order.description: Sort order used for this query

selector.description: The selector used for this query (echoed back)

next_commit.description:
SHA of the next commit for pagination. None if there are no more commits.
- For `order=desc`: use `until=next_commit` to get the next page
- For `order=asc`: use `since=next_commit` to get the next page

tree.description: The ID of the tree pointed to by this commit

oneOf.title: Range
oneOf.description: Select commits in a range (like git's A..B)

Range.description: Select commits in a range (like git's A..B)

since.description: Exclude this commit and its ancestors

oneOf.title: Since
oneOf.description: Select commits since this commit SHA (exclusive)

Since.description: Select commits since this commit SHA (exclusive)

oneOf.title: Until
oneOf.description: Select commits until this commit SHA (inclusive)

Until.description: Select commits until this commit SHA (inclusive)

CommitsSelector.title: CommitsSelector
CommitsSelector.description: Selector for commits based on since/until

ComparisonStatus.description: The status of a commit comparison

githubRepoName.description: The GitHub repository name in "owner/repo" format

sha.description:
The SHA of the commit this branch should point to.
If not provided, the branch will be created from the default branch.

name.description: The name of the created branch

sha.description: The SHA of the commit the branch points to

files.description: Files to add, modify, or delete in this commit

branch.description:
The branch to commit to (e.g., "main"). If not provided, commits to the default branch.
If the branch does not exist, it is created as an orphan branch with this commit as its root.

author.description: Optional author information (if not provided, uses default)

expectedSha.description:
Optional expected parent SHA for optimistic concurrency control.
If provided, the commit will only be created if the current branch tip (or parent) matches this SHA.
This prevents race conditions when multiple commits are being created concurrently.
Returns a conflict error if the check fails.

tree.description: The SHA of the tree for this commit

parents.description: The SHAs of the parent commits (empty array for initial commit)

author.description: Optional author information (if not provided, uses committer info)

committer.description: Optional committer information (if not provided, uses default)

files.title: Files
files.description: A map of file names to their contents.

rev.description:
The revision (branch, tag, or commit hash) to checkout from the source repo.

This field supersedes 'branch'.

The revision (branch, tag, or commit hash) to checkout from the source repo.
The given revision will be used as the new repository's default branch.

This field supersedes 'branch'.

allBranches.description:
If true, all branches and tags will be fetched from the source repository.
Defaults to false, which only fetches the default branch (unless `rev` is set).

name.description:
This name is not visible to users, and is only accessible to you via API and in the
dashboard. Mostly useful for observability.

defaultBranch.description: The default branch name for the repository. Defaults to "main" if not specified.

source.description: Fork from another Git repository. Cannot be used with `import`.

import.description: Import static content with an initial commit. Cannot be used with `source`.

persistence.description: Defaults to persistent. If set to sticky we will intelligently clean it up as your account reaches its limits.

ports.description:
Optional list of ports to expose externally. If not provided, port 3000
will be exposed on port 443 by default. Pass an empty array to disable
external ports. Only ports 8081 and 443 can be configured externally for
now. Any target port is allowed.

waitForReadySignal.description:
Whether the api request should wait for the VM to be ready before
returning. By default, the VM is considered ready when the serial
console is ready for login.

readySignalTimeoutSeconds.description:
How long to wait for the ready signal before timing out. Defaults to 120
seconds if not provided.

workdir.description:
Optional working directory for the VM. File system and shell commands
will be executed in this directory.

persistence.description: Persistence strategy for the VM. If not provided, defaults to 'sticky' with priority 5.

systemd.description: Optional systemd configuration for services to run in the VM.

git.description: Git configuration for the VM, including user settings and repositories to clone into the VM at startup.

recreate.description:
If true, the VM can be recreated if it is deleted. The VM will keep the same ID
and be recreated with the same configuration when something tries to start it.

aptDeps.description:
Optional list of apt packages to install when setting up the VM.
These packages will be installed using `apt-get install` on VM startup.

domains.description:
Optional list of custom domains to map to this VM. Each domain can optionally
specify a vm port. If vm_port is not specified, defaults to 443.
Domains must be verified and owned by the account before they can be mapped.

snapshotId.description: Debugging tool, if you believe its base is incorrect you can go off it.

oneOf.title: Git
oneOf.description: `dir` is the Directory to deploy from. If not provided, the root of the repository will be used.

DiffFile.description: A file that was changed in the comparison

sha.description: The blob SHA of the file

status.description: The status of the file (added, removed, modified, renamed, copied, changed, unchanged)

DiffFileStatus.description: The status of a file in a diff

DynamicConfig.description: Dynamic route configuration for paths that should be handled by the worker

source.description: Source pattern (regex or glob pattern like "/api/.*" or "/api/**")

methods.description: HTTP methods this route applies to (if None, applies to all methods)

Error_DomainMappingError.description:
The structure of an error - should rarely be interacted with directly.

Create your own error types, implement [`ServiceError`] for them, and they will automatically
convert to [`Error`] with `?` or `.into()`.

path.description: The path of the file in the repository (e.g., "src/main.rs")

content.description:
The content of the file. Mutually exclusive with `deleted`.
If encoding is base64, this should be a base64-encoded string.

encoding.description: The encoding of the content. Defaults to utf8.

deleted.description: Whether to delete this file. Mutually exclusive with `content`.

domains.description: ID of the project to deploy, if not provided will create a new project

envVars.description: The environment variables that the cloudstate deploy can access

domain.description: The domain to create a verification code for

timeout.description: The amount of milliseconds after with no traffic we kill your server. For rate limiting purposes we count any started server as active for the 5 seconds after the last request.

staticDir.description:
Directory containing static files to be served directly (e.g., ".next/static", "_next/static").
Files are served at the URL path specified by static_path_prefix (defaults to root "/").

staticPathPrefix.description:
URL path prefix where static files are served (e.g., "/_next/static"). Defaults to "/" (root).
When set, only requests matching this prefix will be served from static_dir.

publicDir.description:
Directory containing public files to be served at the root of the domain (e.g., "public").
These files are served without any path prefix - a file at public/favicon.ico is served at /favicon.ico.

prerenderDir.description:
Directory containing prerendered HTML files (e.g., ".next/standalone/.next/server/app" for Next.js).
Files are served as: ${prerender_dir}/${path}.html or ${prerender_dir}/index.html for root.

staticOnly.description: Set to true for static-only deployments (no dynamic server/entrypoint required)

rewrites.description: Rewrites (internal URL rewrites with capture groups)

dynamic.description: Dynamic routes that should be handled by the worker

cleanUrls.description:
When true, all HTML files will have their extension removed. Visiting a path with .html will redirect to extensionless path (308).
For example, a static file named about.html will be served when visiting /about. Visiting /about.html will redirect to /about.

trailingSlash.description:
When false, visiting a path with a trailing slash will redirect (308) to path without trailing slash.
When true, opposite behavior occurs. When None (default), no automatic redirects based on trailing slash.

experimental.description: Experimental feature flags. Avoid relying on these for long-term API stability.

files.description:
The files to deploy, a map of file paths to file contents, e.g. { \"index.js\": {\"content\": \"your main\", \"encoding\": \"utf-8\"}, \"file2.js\": {\"content\": \"your helper\" } }

**Do not include node modules in this bundle, they will not work**. Instead, includes a package-lock.json, bun.lockb, pnpm-lock.yaml, or yarn.lock, the node modules for the project will be installed from that lock file, or use the node_modules field in the configuration to specify the node modules to install.

entrypoint.description:
The entrypoint file for the website. If not specified we try to automatically detect it.
For static-only deployments (static: true), this will be None.

domain.description: The domain to create a verification code for

script.description: The JavaScript or TypeScript script to execute

envVars.description: The environment variables to set for the script

nodeModules.description: The node modules to install for the script

tags.description: Tags for you to organize your scripts, useful for tracking what you're running

peerDependencyResolution.description: If false, we'll not resolve peer dependencies for the packages given, this can speed up execute performance, but will break packages with peers unless the peers are manually specified.

customHeaders.description: These headers will be added to every fetch request made through the script

proxy.description: Proxy all outgoing requests through this URL

zeroRetention.description: If true, Freestyle will not retain the code, any logs, environment variables, or results from this execution.

result.description: The return value of the default export of the script

encoding.description: The encoding of the file. Either **utf-8** or **base64**

executable.description: Whether the file should be marked executable after being written

FreestyleVerifyDomainRequest.description: Verify a domain verification request, can either be done for a domain, or for a specific request

vmInstanceId.description: Unique ID for the current VM run (new ID generated on each start/resume)

sha.description: The hash / object ID of the file.
The hash / object ID of the directory.
The hash / object ID of the file.
The hash / object ID of the directory.

GitReference.description: A reference to a Git object

name.description: The name of the ref (e.g., "refs/heads/main" or "refs/tags/v1.0.0")

sha.description: The SHA-1 hash of the Git object this reference points to

repo.description: url or id of the git repository

path.description: path to place the repo on

allowedUsers.description:
List of allowed Linux users. If null, identity can SSH as any user.
If specified, identity can only SSH as users in this list.

HeaderConfig.description: Header configuration for setting custom headers on matching paths

headers.description: Headers to set (array of key-value pairs)

gid.description: Optional fixed GID; if None, allocate

uid.description: Optional fixed UID; if None, allocate

gecos.description: GECOS field (descriptive string, e.g., full name)

groups.description: Groups to add user to (all groups use 'm' membership lines)

home.description: Home directory path (optional, defaults to /home/{username} for regular users, / for system users)

shell.description: Login shell (optional, defaults to /bin/bash for regular users, /usr/sbin/nologin for system users)

Public_DomainMappingError.description:
Public API error wrapper. Mark public APIs with `Public<T>` to ensure private error
details aren't exposed.

RedirectConfig.description: Redirect configuration for permanent or temporary redirects (Vercel-compatible)

source.description: Source pattern (can be exact path or regex)

statusCode.description:
HTTP status code for redirect (301, 302, 307, 308). Takes precedence over `permanent`.
If neither `status_code` nor `permanent` is provided, defaults to 308.

permanent.description:
If true, uses 308 (permanent redirect). If false, uses 307 (temporary redirect).
Ignored if `status_code` is explicitly set. Vercel-compatible alternative to `status_code`.

RewriteConfig.description: Rewrite configuration for URL rewrites (internal, not visible to client)

destination.description: Destination path with optional capture group substitution ($1, $2, etc.)

date.description: The date marker for this signature

SnapshotId.description:
Branded snapshot ID in the format `sc-<20 lowercase alphanumeric chars>`.
Unlike `VmShortId`, this is a plain `String` wrapper with no fixed-size array or padding.

sourceVmId.description: The ID of the source VM

accountId.description: Account ID of the creator (if available)

deleted.description: Whether the snapshot has been soft-deleted

vcpuCount.description: Number of vCPUs configured at snapshot time

memSizeMib.description: Memory size in MiB configured at snapshot time

SnapshotLayerId.description: Branded snapshot layer ID — always a 16-character alphanumeric lowercase string.

oneOf.description:
When storage quota is reached, the least recently used snapshots will be
deleted. Higher priority = less likely to be evicted. Range 0-10, default 5.
The snapshot will be stored until manually deleted.

persistence.description: Persistence strategy for the snapshot. Defaults to Sticky with priority 5.

sourceVmId.description: The ID of the source VM

sourceVmInstanceId.description: The instance ID of the source VM

id.description: The ID of the VM that was suspended

vmInstanceId.description: The ID of the VM instance that was suspended

snapshotLayerId.description: The ID of the snapshot layer created for this VM — if a layer was created

exec.description: Executable to run (can specify multiple commands that run sequentially).

user.description: Linux user to run the service as.

group.description: Linux group to run the service in.

after.description:
Establishes an ordering dependency. The current unit will start only
after the units listed in After= have started. This is useful for
ensuring that certain services are up and running before the current
service begins its operation.

requires.description:
Establishes a strong dependency. If the required unit fails to
start or stops unexpectedly, the current unit will also be stopped. This
ensures that a service critical to the functioning of the current unit
is running and stable. Units listed in Requires= are activated along
with the current unit. If the required unit is not active, systemd will
attempt to start it. This directive signifies a tight coupling between
services, where the current service cannot function without the required
service.

onFailure.description:
Units to activate when this unit enters a failed state. This is useful
for triggering recovery actions, notifications, or cleanup services when
the current service fails.

enable.description:
Whether to enable this service (calls `systemctl enable <service>`).
When enabled, the service will start automatically at boot.

deleteAfterSuccess.description: For oneshot: remove unit on success.

remainAfterExit.description:
For oneshot: remain active after exit (default: true).
When false, the service can be started again even if it already ran.

exec.description: Executable to run (can specify multiple commands that run sequentially).

user.description: Linux user to run the service as.

group.description: Linux group to run the service in.

after.description:
Establishes an ordering dependency. The current unit will start only
after the units listed in After= have started. This is useful for
ensuring that certain services are up and running before the current
service begins its operation.

requires.description:
Establishes a strong dependency. If the required unit fails to
start or stops unexpectedly, the current unit will also be stopped. This
ensures that a service critical to the functioning of the current unit
is running and stable. Units listed in Requires= are activated along
with the current unit. If the required unit is not active, systemd will
attempt to start it. This directive signifies a tight coupling between
services, where the current service cannot function without the required
service.

onFailure.description:
Units to activate when this unit enters a failed state. This is useful
for triggering recovery actions, notifications, or cleanup services when
the current service fails.

enable.description:
Whether to enable this service (calls `systemctl enable <service>`).
When enabled, the service will start automatically at boot.

deleteAfterSuccess.description: For oneshot: remove unit on success.

tagger.description: The tagger who created the tag

target.description: The object this tag points to

allowedUsers.description:
List of allowed Linux users. If null, identity can SSH as any user.
If specified, identity can only SSH as users in this list.

VmDomainConfig.description: Domain configuration for a VM, specifying the domain and optional target port

domain.description: The domain name to map to the VM

vmPort.description: The port on the VM to route traffic to. Defaults to 443 if not specified.

VmId.description:
VM ID — always 20 alphanumeric lowercase characters.
New IDs are fully random. Legacy short IDs are right-padded with '0' on parse.

vmInstanceId.description: Unique ID for the current VM run (new ID generated on each start/resume)

snapshotId.description: The snapshot this VM was created from, if any.

oneOf.description:
When your storage quota is reached, the least recently used VMs will be
deleted.

priority.description:
Priority for eviction when storage quota is reached. Higher values
mean the VM is less likely to be evicted. Range is 0-10, default is
5.

oneOf.description:
The VM will be deleted after the idle timeout. It's not guaranteed that
the VM will be deleted immediately.

snapshotId.description:
Optional snapshot ID to create a VM from. If provided, the new VM will be
created from the specified snapshot. Cannot be used together with fork_vm_id or docker_image.

baseImage.description:
Optional inline Dockerfile content to build and import a custom image from.
Cannot be used together with snapshot_id.
The image is built inside a temporary sandbox VM (no build context is used).

workdir.description: Optional working directory for the VM. If not provided, the default to '/'

idleTimeoutSeconds.description:
Idle timeout in seconds. If set, the VM will be automatically suspended
after this many seconds of no network activity. Defaults to 300 seconds
(5 minutes) if not provided or the last used timeout for the forked VM.

persistence.description:
Persistence strategy for the VM. If not provided, defaults to 'sticky'
with priority 5.

ports.description:
Optional list of ports to expose externally. If not provided, port 3000
will be exposed on port 443 by default. Pass an empty array to disable
external ports. Only ports 8081 and 443 can be configured externally for
now. Any target port is allowed.

users.description: Linux users to create on VM startup

groups.description: Linux groups to create on VM startup

git.description: Repositories to clone and git options for the VM.

discriminator.description: Optional discriminator to differentiate snapshots with otherwise identical configurations

aptDeps.description:
Optional list of apt packages to install when setting up the VM.
These packages will be installed using `apt-get install` on VM startup.

skipCache.description:
When true, bypasses the snapshot cache and always creates a new snapshot.
The new snapshot still stores the template hash, so it becomes the updated cache entry
for future requests that do not set skip_cache.

result.description: The return value of the default export of the script

tags.name: Web
tags.description: APIs for deploying websites. We handle node modules caching, scaling, certificates and the whole end to end process. Send the code using the [deploy](#tag/web/POST/web/v1/deploy) endpoint, and you'll get a full hosted website back. Works with any TypeScript or JavaScript codebase.

tags.name: Execute
tags.description: APIs for running code. Send the code using the [execute](#tag/execute/POST/execute/v1/execute) endpoint, and you'll get the output back. Works with any TypeScript or JavaScript code + handles any node modules and environment variables you want.

tags.name: Domains
tags.description:
APIs for managing domains. This is only relevant when you want to start to deploy to custom domains. 
Please read [this guide](https://github.com/freestyle-sh/sandbox_sdks/blob/main/docs/custom_domains.md) to understand how deployments work with custom domains.

tags.name: Git
tags.description: APIs for managing git repositories and accessing git objects like commits, trees, blobs, tags, and refs.

tags.name: Identity
tags.description: APIs for managing identities and access tokens.

tags.name: VM
tags.description: APIs for managing lightweight virtual machines (VMs) to run your code in isolated environments.

tags.name: Deprecated
tags.description: Deprecated APIs that are planned to be removed in future versions.
